programs.name: Michigan Choice Waiver Program
programs.covers: Adult day health, chore services, community health worker, community living supports, community transportation, counseling, environmental accessibility adaptations, fiscal intermediary, goods and services, home delivered meals, nursing services, personal emergency response systems, private duty nursing/respiratory care[7]
programs.notes: Income limits from non-.gov sources for 2025/2026 ($2,982/month per applicant, regardless of marital status)[1][4][8]; official .gov confirms income/asset criteria but no exact amounts[7]. Age from non-.gov[4][5]; .gov implies adults[7]. No official .gov verification for exact income, benefit value, wait time, or application URL. Phone from agency site[8]. Services from official .gov[7].

programs.name: Medicaid for the Aged, Blind and Disabled (ABD)
programs.covers: comprehensive package of health care benefits including vision, dental, and mental health services
programs.notes: No search results from official .gov sources provide specific details on Michigan's 'Medicaid for the Aged, Blind and Disabled (ABD)' program income limits, benefit values, phone number, or wait times. General Medicaid info found on michigan.gov/mdhhs, but ABD-specific facts unverified. Income limits vary by Medicaid category (e.g., nursing home ~$2,982 single in 2026 from non-.gov[2]; medically needy $1,330 single[2]). Min age inferred as typical for aged category. Application from general MDHHS page[8].

programs.name: Michigan Medicare Savings Programs (QMB, SLMB, QI)
programs.benefit_value: Varies by program: QMB pays Part B premium, copayments, deductibles; SLMB pays Part B premium only; QI (ALMB) pays Part B premium when funding available[1][4][5][6]
programs.covers: Medicare Part B premiums (all); copayments and deductibles (QMB only)[1][4][5][6]
programs.notes: No official Michigan .gov source in results provides exact 2026 income limits, minimum age, phone, application URL, or wait times for single/couple. Limits based on % FPL (QMB ≤100%, SLMB 100-120%, QI 120-135%) but exact $ amounts outdated or national. Resource limits $9,950 single/$14,910 couple for QMB/SLMB/QI[1][4]. Medicare eligibility generally requires age 65+ or disabled[5].

programs.name: MI Options Counseling (SHIP/Medicare Counseling)
programs.benefit_value: Free counseling services; no monetary benefit amount
programs.covers: Person-Centered Options Counseling (long-term care options, community services, supports); Medicare Counseling (plan options, costs, Extra Help, prescription drugs, billing, benefits explanation)
programs.notes: Program provides free counseling available to any adult regardless of income, assets, age (18+ implied), or disability status. No income/asset limits, monetary benefits, or application process required. MI Choice Waiver (mentioned in other results) is a separate Medicaid program with income limits.

programs.name: Michigan Medicare Assistance Program (MMAP)
programs.covers: Free counseling and assistance with Medicare questions, plan choices, prescription drug coverage, Medicare Savings Programs, and other health insurance issues
programs.notes: MMAP is a state health insurance counseling program (SHIP), not a benefits program with income/age limits or fixed benefit values. Search results discuss Michigan Medicaid and Medicare Savings Programs (MSPs) but no official .gov source confirms exact MMAP eligibility limits or benefit schedules. Phone from state site; coverage description synthesized from program purpose. MSP income limits (e.g., QMB ~$1,350 single[5][6]) are federal/state programs MMAP helps apply for, not MMAP itself.

programs.name: LifeCircles PACE
programs.benefit_value: Complete health care coverage (no cost for Medicaid-eligible participants)
programs.covers: Comprehensive integrated Medicare and Medicaid services for frail elderly meeting nursing home level of care criteria; services delivered through community-based model
programs.notes: Income limit of $2,982/month is for Long Term Care Medicaid (2026)[3]. No couple income limit found in search results. Benefit value is 'no cost' for Medicaid-eligible participants; actual dollar value of services not specified in available sources[8]. Phone number verified from LifeCircles PACE directly[4]. No direct application URL or wait time information located. Service area limited to Muskegon County and portions of Ottawa and Allegan Counties[4]. Official state PACE page exists but does not contain LifeCircles-specific details[7].

programs.name: SNAP Food Assistance (Michigan Bridge Card)
programs.benefit_value: Maximum monthly allotments vary by household size (e.g., $298 for 1 person, $546 for 2 people); actual amount based on net income, not fixed[5][7]
programs.covers: Benefits for purchasing eligible food items using Michigan Bridge Card[8]
programs.notes: No official Michigan .gov sources in results provide exact income limits, benefit schedules, phone, application URL, or wait times; values from non-.gov sites like snapscreener.com[1] and propel.app[2] match closely but unverified from government; USDA general data used for allotments[5]; official MDHHS page lacks specifics[8]

programs.name: MI Home Heating Credit (LIHEAP)
programs.benefit_value: Varies based on income, household size, and heating costs; up to $500 maximum for single person (older data); standard allowance examples: $604 for 0-1 exemptions[3]
programs.covers: Helps pay home heating expenses for qualified Michigan homeowners or renters via refund or energy draft[6]
programs.notes: MI Home Heating Credit is a state tax credit (MI-1040CR-7), not the federal LIHEAP grant program; income limits are annual (e.g., Table A 2025: $17,243 for 0-1, $23,271 for 2[3]), not monthly; no fixed benefit value or age requirement specified; data from 2025 tables, verify for 2026; LIHEAP has separate monthly limits (e.g., $1,434 single[1])[6][3]

programs.name: State Rehabilitation Services - Weatherization
programs.benefit_value: No cost to eligible residents; services based on whole home energy audit (no fixed dollar amount specified)
programs.covers: Energy conservation measures (e.g., insulation, air sealing), health & safety services determined by whole home energy audit
programs.notes: Program is Michigan's Weatherization Assistance Program (WAP), administered by MDHHS; eligibility at or below 200% Federal Poverty Guidelines or categorical (e.g., recent Title IV/XVI cash assistance), but exact monthly income limits for single/couple not specified in official sources. No 'State Rehabilitation Services - Weatherization' exact match found; facts from WAP official pages. No application URL, phone, age req., or wait times in results.

programs.name: AgeWays Caregiver Assistance (Respite Care)
programs.wait_time: 20–30 minutes on the phone for initial eligibility screening[8]
programs.covers: Respite care funds up to $575 via care vouchers for caregivers of those 60+, with professional referral[7]
programs.notes: No official .gov sources found for 'AgeWays Caregiver Assistance (Respite Care)' program details like income limits, age, or benefit schedules specific to respite care. AgeWays.org (non-.gov) mentions MI Choice Medicaid Waiver with 2026 income limit $2,982/month (single implied), assets $9,950 individual/$14,910 couple, age 65+ or disabled adult needing nursing home level care[1]. Respite program at caregiverrespiteprogram.org offers up to $575 vouchers, requires referral, for caregivers of 60+ providing 2+ hours/week[7]. Phone from AgeWays FAQ[4]. Cannot verify exact program match or requested facts from official government sources.

programs.name: MI Options Person-Centered Options Counseling (Respite)
programs.covers: Exploring long-term care and support options for adults, including home- and community-based services; identifying next steps after major life events; building actionable steps for options respecting client preferences; Medicare counseling (enrollment, coordination with Medicaid, cost savings like Extra Help, prescription drug options, billing, benefits explanation)[3][5]
programs.notes: MI Options Person-Centered Options Counseling is available to any adult regardless of income, assets, or disability status per official MDHHS source[3]. No income limits, age minimum, fixed benefit value, or wait times specified. 'Respite' appears associated with MI Choice Waiver (which covers respite services[2][4][7]), not directly with MI Options counseling. No official .gov application URL or exact benefit dollar amounts found for this specific program. Phone from WellWise Services (MI Options provider)[5]. MI Choice Waiver has separate eligibility (e.g., income ~$2,901/month in 2025[1], age 18+[2]).

programs.name: Meals on Wheels (via Area Agencies on Aging)
programs.wait_time: Usually within a few days if eligible; assessments at six-month intervals[1]
programs.covers: Home-delivered hot meals Monday-Friday (some weekend/frozen meals); nutrition services; daily wellness checks[1][3][5]
programs.notes: No official .gov sources in results confirm income limits, benefit values, statewide phone, application URL, or source URL. Program administered locally via Area Agencies on Aging (AAAs) in Michigan; eligibility primarily age 60+, homebound, unable to shop/prepare meals; no financial requirements stated across sources[1][3][5][6]. Varies by AAA/provider; contact local AAA for specifics.

programs.name: Senior Community Service Employment Program (SCSEP)
programs.benefit_value: Paid at Michigan state minimum wage per hour, bi-weekly for actual time worked[3]
programs.covers: Paid community service job training, employment assistance[10]
programs.notes: No official .gov sources provide current (2026) Michigan-specific monthly income limits, exact benefit dollar values, phone, application URL, wait times, or full eligibility page. Income eligibility is 125% federal poverty level (annual, outdated 2021 figures in [1]); min age 55 confirmed across sources[2][3][7][9]. Michigan MDHHS docs [5][8] affirm federal guidelines but lack specifics. No monthly limits found; convert annual at own risk.

programs.name: Legal Aid of Western Michigan
programs.benefit_value: Free legal services (no fixed dollar value; services provided at no cost to eligible clients)
programs.covers: housing, family law, public benefits defense, consumer cases, education
programs.notes: No official .gov sources in results confirm details; program is nonprofit (lawestmi.org). Income at or below 125% Federal Poverty Guidelines (exact $ amounts not specified and vary yearly); assets <$15,000. Seniors (60+) eligible beyond normal income limits. No .gov eligibility page, application URL, wait times, or min age found. Phone from michiganlegalhelp.org.

programs.name: Long-Term Care Ombudsman Program
programs.covers: Advocacy and assistance for residents of long-term care facilities (nursing homes, homes for the aged, adult foster care homes) by investigating complaints, providing information, and conducting facility visits to improve quality of care and life.
programs.notes: The Long-Term Care Ombudsman Program (MLTCOP) in Michigan is a free advocacy service mandated by federal law. It does not provide financial benefits, have income/asset limits, age requirements, benefit values, applications, or waitlists for participants. It serves residents of long-term care facilities regardless of financial status. Search results confuse it with Medicaid-funded long-term care programs like MI Choice Waiver. All facts set to null as they do not apply to this program per official descriptions [7][8][9].

programs.name: Senior Companion Program
programs.benefit_value: Tax-exempt hourly stipend (exact amount not specified in sources)
programs.covers: Provides meaningful retirement opportunities for volunteers age 55+ by assisting frail older adults and adults with disabilities with person-to-person support; volunteers receive tax-exempt stipend, mileage reimbursement, meal assistance, training, insurance, and recognition
programs.notes: No official Michigan government (.gov) sources found describing the Senior Companion Program's eligibility, income limits, or benefits. Information from non-government sites (e.g., [2][4][5][6]) consistently indicates minimum age 55 and low-income requirement (typically ≤200% federal poverty level with medical deductions), but exact monthly income limits for single/couple, benefit dollar value, phone, application URL, source URL, and wait time unverified from official sources. Program is federally supported via AmeriCorps Seniors, run by local agencies in MI.

programs.name: Homestead Property Tax Credit
programs.benefit_value: Up to $1,900 maximum credit limit (computed as percentage of property taxes or rent exceeding 3.2% of income, with phase-out and increases for age 65+ or disabled)[2][7]
programs.covers: Portion of property taxes for qualified Michigan homeowners and renters (based on taxes/23% of rent exceeding 3.2% of total household resources)[2][6]
programs.notes: Income limits are **annual** Total Household Resources (THR): $71,500 maximum for 2025 (phase-out starts at $62,500); no monthly or household-size-specific breakdowns verified. No minimum age requirement found. Claim filed with MI-1040CR form via state income tax return[1][2][6]. Data reflects 2025 tax year info from official MI Treasury sources; older results show prior years (e.g., $60,000 in 2019)[1].

programs.name: Senior Services via Area Agencies on Aging
programs.notes: No official .gov sources in search results describe a specific program named 'Senior Services via Area Agencies on Aging' in Michigan with the requested eligibility details. Results mention related programs like MI Choice Waiver (administered via Area Agencies on Aging) with varying income limits (e.g., $2,901/month single[2][4], $2,982/month[5]) but no uniform facts matching the query, no benefit schedules, contacts, or application portals from .gov sites.
